text:
Market today: Risk-on mood, driven by the Iran deal (oil stabilizing, fear dropping) and China's stronger factory data boosting tech export names. That combo is a tailwind for chips and growth tech - exactly your book. Watch for the yen weakness ripple; Japan intervention risk can spike volatility fast.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one to watch - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF that decays daily, and your loss is already meaningful; stop at $200 but a bounce here doesn't mean the problem is fixed. SPCX (+0.53%) is brand-new, very volatile IPO with thin data - margin amplifies any sudden drop hard. SMH (-0.69%) is mildly underwater; less urgent but tied to SOXL's same chip risk.

Watch: 1) SOXL $200 stop - do not let it slide there on margin without a plan. 2) Chip names broadly - China tech export demand is a real catalyst; watch if SMH breaks higher off this news.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is SOXL on margin - get clear on your exit plan before the session heats up.
plays: SOXL: Your stop is $200 - if you're on margin and uncomfortable with a 3x decay name down 7%, consider trimming size now rather than waiting for the stop to get hit.
SPCX: Brand-new IPO, thin data, stop $150 - keep position small and manage manually; margin makes a gap-down here disproportionately painful.
SMH: No new add yet - watch if China export demand news pushes it back toward flat; a clear reclaim of breakeven would be the signal to hold with more confidence.
